preview_text:
The model rates this fixture as highly competitive, with Germany favored at 36% to win but by a narrow margin over Ecuador at 33%. The two-point gap suggests roughly even underlying strength, with Ecuador assigned a credible underdog probability rather than a distant outsider role.

The draw probability of 32% indicates the model expects a closely matched contest where a stalemate remains a plausible outcome. Win probabilities are distributed across all three results without a dominant favourite, pointing to genuine uncertainty about the match's decisive shape.

The market reads the fixture very differently. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both assign Germany roughly 64% to win—a 28-point gap above the model's estimate—while compressing Ecuador to 17% and draws to 19%. The model and market are substantially misaligned on the favourite's strength. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the divergence is large enough to merit scrutiny of underlying assumptions.
